title: Aria and the Mystery Doodle
description: Theo draws a picture and asks Aria to guess what it is. She gets it wrong — and that turns out to be the most useful part of the game.

steps.content: Theo drew a round shape with two triangles on top and held it up. 'A cat!' Aria said right away, lights blinking proudly. 'Nope,' Theo grinned. 'It's a slice of pizza. Those are pepperoni.'

steps.question: Why did Aria guess 'cat' instead of 'pizza'?
options: She was trying to trick Theo
Round shapes with triangles on top usually mean cat

steps.content: Aria wasn't lying, and she wasn't broken. She looked at the shape and picked whatever it reminded her of most. This time it just reminded her of the wrong thing.

steps.instruction: Theo drew something new. This time Aria said, 'Maybe a snowman... or a robot... or a sandcastle? Not totally sure.' What's the best thing for Theo to do?
options: Tell her she has to pick just one
Tell her which one is right
Ask her why she thinks it could be a snowman

steps.content: 'Sandcastle,' said Theo. 'But you weren't sure, and that's the part I liked.' Aria's lights flickered. 'Sounding sure and being right aren't the same thing. Guess I should say when I'm not.'
